talk_metadata.talk_title: Integrating Digital Pathology and AI with Molecular Pathology: Opportunities and Challenges
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we're at the very peak of inflated expectations"
Speaker said: "I predict it will go even higher"
Speaker said: "we won't replace MSI testing with an H&E predictor"
Speaker said: "the number of less than optimal samples that come to pathology is extremely large, and the expectation that foundation models ... are going to be able to handle ... is a little naive"
Speaker said: "we need rigorous performance metrics more than we're currently using"

key_data_gaps: No prospective clinical trial data presented for AI‑based mutation prediction
No head‑to‑head comparison with standard molecular assays
Limited discussion of performance on low‑quality or cytology specimens

crispro_opportunity.description: Many AI models show modest AUCs (0.6‑0.7) for mutation prediction; a high‑confidence, CrisPRO‑validated biomarker panel could stratify patients for targeted testing.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted: "the best predictor ... is MSI with an AUC of about 0.9, but for NRAS or PIK3CA it falls to 0.5‑0.6"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate multi‑modal embeddings and apply rigorous cross‑validation to improve predictive power beyond current AUROCs.

crispro_opportunity.description: Confounding factors such as biopsy type and tumor heterogeneity cause false positives; CrisPRO could model these variables to isolate true mutation signals.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "fluid samples and small biopsies drive ALK‑positive calls even when morphology is uninformative"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO’s probabilistic framework can weight sample quality metrics, reducing bias from low‑quality slides.

crispro_opportunity.description: Spatial transcriptomics and multiplex IF generate rich data; partnering with CrisPRO to fuse these modalities could create next‑generation predictive biomarkers.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker described: "combining immunofluorescence with transcriptomics and H&E ... could predict immune checkpoint response"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO’s multimodal integration engine can jointly analyze H&E embeddings, IF protein panels, and spatial transcriptomics to derive composite scores.

cited_competitors.name: PathAI
cited_competitors.context: mentioned as a vendor providing quantitative IHC tools

cited_competitors.name: Singular Genomics
cited_competitors.context: highlighted for higher throughput spatial transcriptomics platform

cited_competitors.name: ProvGigaPath
cited_competitors.context: cited as a foundation model used for mutation prediction

cited_competitors.name: Campanella et al. (Mount Sinai/Sloan Kettering)
cited_competitors.context: referenced for fine‑tuning a foundation model to improve EGFR prediction

unresolved_questions: How can AI models achieve clinical‑grade sensitivity and specificity for low‑prevalence mutations?
What standards and benchmarks are needed for AI‑based diagnostic tools?
How will spatial transcriptomics add actionable information beyond conventional NGS?

assets_to_track: Foundation models for pathology (e.g., ProvGigaPath, CONCH)
Spatial transcriptomics platforms (10x Visium, Singular Genomics)
